title: Senior Recruiter, QA Track
summary: Own the candidate side of our QA practice — sourcing, vetting, prepping, and standing behind QA engineers placed into client engagements. This is a long-arc role for someone who already knows the difference between a manual tester and a contract-testing zealot, and who'd rather place ten engineers carefully than fifty hastily.
responsibilities: Source and vet QA candidates across automation, performance, and SDET-level work — primarily Selenium, Playwright, Cypress, REST Assured, JMeter, and K6.
Run technical screens that go beyond keyword matching. We expect a working understanding of contract testing, flake triage, and modern test architecture.
Build and maintain relationships with a long-term bench. Most of our best placements come from candidates we've known for two-plus years.
Prep candidates for client interviews with substance, not scripts.
Negotiate rates honestly, in plain English, on both sides of the table.
Stay engaged with placed engineers through the six-month check-in and beyond.
Collaborate with the Java track recruiter and Operations Lead on cross-staffed engagements.
qualifications: 5+ years recruiting in IT staffing or in-house tech recruiting, with at least 2 years focused on QA roles.
Working vocabulary in QA tooling — you should be able to tell a candidate's story to a hiring manager without leaning on the resume.
Track record of placements that stuck. We'll ask for retention numbers.
Calm, deliberate communication style. We are not a firm of high-volume cold callers.
Comfortable with vendor-network work (MSP/VMS) and direct client relationships.
niceToHave: Background in QA before recruiting (yes, really — we've hired a few of these, they're excellent).
Experience with regulated industries (healthcare, financial services, insurance).
Existing network of senior QA engineers in the US tech market.
compensationNote: Base + commission, no draw games. We share comp bands openly during the second conversation.

title: Recruiter, Java Track
summary: Run the candidate pipeline for our Java practice — mid-to-senior backend engineers across Spring, microservices, and platform work. You'll work alongside our QA-track recruiter, sharing a bench, a discipline, and a refusal to spam the market.
responsibilities: Source mid-to-senior Java engineers across Spring Boot, Kafka, JPA, AWS, and Kubernetes stacks.
Conduct first-pass technical screens with enough depth to know which engineers we should and shouldn't put forward.
Manage candidate relationships through the full arc: intake, submission, interview prep, offer, start, and check-in.
Write submission notes that hiring managers actually read — short, specific, on-point.
Coordinate with our Operations Lead on contract paperwork, onboarding, and timesheet rhythm.
Show up at the six-month check-in for engineers you placed.
qualifications: 3+ years recruiting Java engineers in the US market, agency or in-house.
Comfortable reading a Java resume — you should be able to spot a Spring Boot engineer who hasn't actually used Spring Boot since 2019.
Strong written communication. Most of our submissions are read before any call happens.
Honest relationship style. We don't do hard closes.
Familiar with contract-to-hire, direct placement, and pure contract engagements.
niceToHave: Background in Java development or technical project management.
Experience working with VMS systems (Beeline, Fieldglass, etc.) without losing your soul.
Existing relationships with mid-market and enterprise hiring managers.
compensationNote: Base + commission. Bands shared during the second conversation; the floor is comfortably above the agency-recruiter mean.

title: Interview Helper — Senior Java Engineer
summary: Part-time contract role for a senior Java engineer who wants to spend a few hours a week helping our candidates prep for technical interviews. You'll run mock interviews, give honest feedback, and occasionally sit in as a technical advisor on our intake calls. This is not a recruiting job. It's a craft job.
responsibilities: Run 60-minute mock technical interviews with TSH candidates (mostly Java, occasionally adjacent stacks).
Provide written feedback the candidate can actually act on — what they nailed, what they fumbled, what they should brush up on before the real interview.
Sit in on a small number of intake calls each month to help us assess senior-level candidates.
Stay current enough to coach — Spring Boot, modern testing, system design at the service-architecture level.
Occasionally help us calibrate a hiring manager's bar when we're not sure if their expectations match the rate they're offering.
qualifications: 8+ years of Java engineering experience, with at least 3 at senior or staff level.
Strong fluency in Spring, JVM internals at a working level, distributed systems fundamentals.
Genuine interest in coaching. Not every great engineer wants to do this work; we're looking for the ones who do.
Available for 4-8 hours per week, scheduled in advance.
niceToHave: Prior experience interviewing for FAANG or comparable rigor environments.
Open-source contributions or technical writing we can read.
Background in a regulated industry (extra context for our healthcare and financial services clients).
compensationNote: Hourly contract, paid biweekly. Rate set during the offer conversation based on experience and availability.

title: Operations Lead
summary: Run the unsexy, load-bearing middle of TSH — contracts, payroll cadence, vendor compliance, candidate onboarding paperwork, and the slow accumulation of internal tooling that lets the recruiting team focus on actually recruiting. This is a senior, autonomous role. We'd like you to help shape it.
responsibilities: Own contract execution across direct placements, contract-to-hire, and pure contract engagements.
Manage MSP and VMS onboarding flows for new clients and new candidates.
Run payroll cadence and the small army of edge cases (mid-cycle starts, geographic moves, status changes) that come with a W-2 contract bench.
Coordinate with our outside accounting and legal partners on quarter-close, audit prep, and contract review.
Build and maintain the lightweight internal tooling that keeps the team out of spreadsheets — submission tracking, comp transparency, retention metrics.
Be the calm voice on the line when something at a client gets weird mid-engagement.
qualifications: 5+ years in staffing operations, agency operations, or a comparable people-and-paperwork role at a services firm.
Genuine fluency with employer-of-record obligations: payroll taxes, state UI, workers' comp, basic compliance.
Comfort with VMS systems (Beeline, Fieldglass, SAP Fieldglass, etc.) and MSP relationships.
Documentation reflex. If it happens twice, you write it down. If it happens five times, you tool it.
Calm temperament. You will be the person clients call when something goes sideways.
niceToHave: Light technical background — SQL, basic scripting, comfort in a Notion or Linear-shaped tool.
Experience working across multi-state employment (we hire engineers in most US states).
Curiosity about the engineering side of the work, even if you're not on it.
compensationNote: Salary plus a meaningful operations bonus tied to retention and clean-close metrics. Bands shared during the second conversation.
